c74object.name: weaver~
c74object.digest: Writes incoming track data into a polybuffer~
c74object.description: The `weaver~` object "weaves" audio from external palette buffers into a named `polybuffer~` in real-time. It monitors an incoming time ramp signal on its first inlet. Each track loops individually based on its own length, derived as `main_ramp % track_length`. Track lengths can be manually specified via a list of `[track_id, length]` on the second inlet, or are automatically calculated during consolidation. The object performs sample-accurate weaving at the current location of the input ramp. When a track-specific ramp crosses a bar timestamp (derived from a `bar` buffer), the object performs a lookup in a transcript dictionary. If a corresponding entry exists, it initiates a sample-accurate crossfade to that palette and offset. If the entry exists but the specified palette buffer cannot be found in the Max session, it falls back to a buffer named `stems.[track_id]` and sets the offset to 0. If no dictionary entry exists at all, it automatically crossfades to silence. Transitions and `busy` states are managed per-sample for smooth real-time performance.

inlet.description: Accepts an audio signal treated as the main time ramp in milliseconds. Each track derives its own looping ramp from this signal. Crossings of scheduled bar timestamps and ramp wrap-arounds on the track-specific ramps trigger audio weaving.
Accepts a two-item list consisting of a track ID (integer) and a track length (float/integer) in milliseconds. This updates the looping length for the specified track.

outlet.description: Whenever a track's individual ramp loops (including when the main ramp loops), it outputs an integer corresponding to that track ID.
Sends a bang when the offline consolidation process has finished.
Outputs diagnostic information when the log attribute is enabled.

objarg.name: dictionary
objarg.digest: Name of the dictionary to reference

objarg.name: polybuffer
objarg.digest: Name of the polybuffer~ to reference

method.name: tracks
method.description: Accepts the message `tracks` followed by an integer to set the maximum number of tracks to process.

method.name: clear
method.digest: Reset the object to its initial state
method.description: Resets all track lengths to 0 and clears the internal state, effectively stopping all audio weaving until new track lengths and data are received.

method.name: consolidate
method.description: Runs each track buffer in the referenced polybuffer~ through the same weaving process that would be done in real-time, but as quickly as possible using a simulated ramp from 0 to the end of the song. The total song length and individual track looping lengths are determined from the transcript dictionary. While consolidation is running, real-time attention to the input ramp is paused. Sends a bang to the second outlet when finished, and verbose logging messages to the third outlet.

method.name: list
method.description: Accepts a two-item list [track_id, length] on the second inlet to set the loop length for a specific track in milliseconds.

method.name: visualize
method.description: Sets the `visualize` attribute in real time. When enabled, sends real-time crossfade ramp positions to the visualization script.

method.name: log
method.description: Sets the `log` attribute in real time. When enabled (1), enables verbose logging to the third outlet.

method.name: low
method.description: Sets the `low` attribute in real time, defining the minimum ramp time for crossfades.

method.name: high
method.description: Sets the `high` attribute in real time, defining the maximum ramp time for crossfades.

method.name: tracks
method.description: Sets the `tracks` attribute in real time. (Note: This is already implemented as a method but is listed here for completeness with the other attributes).

attribute.name: visualize
attribute.description: When enabled, sends real-time crossfade ramp positions to the visualization script.

attribute.name: tracks
attribute.description: The maximum number of tracks to process from the transcript dictionary (default is 4).

attribute.name: low
attribute.description: Minimum ramp time for crossfades in milliseconds.

attribute.name: high
attribute.description: Maximum ramp time for crossfades in milliseconds.
